subject: enhance CadiFilter to support more noauthz
commit_message:
enhance CadiFilter to support more noauthz

CadiFilter does hreq.getPathInfo() when getting the current path. This
can return null in the case of just a / in path or based on the
servlet-mapping section of pom (some engines will mark pathinfo as null
for url-pattern with a trailing star for instance). This change strips
out the context path and treats anything after it as what to compare
which makes it immune to changes in url-pattern.

Issue-ID: AAF-726
Change-Id: I7da63b347984dd816a6c24bf876c463a7ee71523
Signed-off-by: DR695H <dr695h@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

We need more info before we pass this.  The code as is could create Null Pointers, for one thing.  Please send note why this is better, for what kinds of Services and documentation or links that show this to be better.

Patch Set 1:

i can certainly tweak for any null pointers. 

As for why this is better, is it removes the need for knowing how the servlet path is defined. An example why is in https://javaee.github.io/servlet-spec/downloads/servlet-3.1/Final/servlet-3_1-final.pdf section 3.5: Servlet Path: The path section that directly corresponds to the mapping which activated this request. This path starts with a ’/’ character except in the case where the request is matched with the ‘/*’ or ““ pattern, in which case it is an empty string.

this means that a servlet path can be very variable, which doesnt match what you would expect often. My fix makes it so you always get the path after the context path, which more closely matches what you do with the tomcat valve and is more consistent.

Patch Set 1:

Any update on this?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

OK, Thank you Daniel for suggestion.

The problem is, that different HTTP Packages do different things, and some do PathINFO, and some do not.  Your suggestion will cover the situation where PathInfo isn't covered like everyone else, but does StringSubstring creations, etc.

I have solved this by trying to get the normal way first, but if it ends up null, then get it your way.

Abandoned

Solution missed some important factors.  However, brought concepts into code where it is safer.
